policy: Every unchecked Sendable declaration in owned production Swift requires a registry entry, an explicit owner, a synchronization invariant, and deterministic evidence.

unsafeDeclarations.invariant: The URLSession reference is assigned once after delegate construction and invalidated only after terminal processing.
All reads and writes occur while the process-local lock is held.
The handshake override is a lock-protected one-way latch.
The benchmark-only one-shot flag is read and written only while locked.
Verbosity is read and written only through the lock-protected property.
The observer token is assigned once during MainActor initialization and read only during deinitialization.

entries.invariant: Ordinary access fails closed after open or migration failure; only explicit Retry serially replaces the failed state after a complete successful reopen.
Ordinary access fails closed after open or migration failure; only explicit Retry serially replaces the failed state after a complete successful reopen.

entries.owner: Qwen3 model generation gate and cache locks
entries.invariant: Mutable MLX values are accessed only by the single generation owner or their documented lock-protected cache.
The provider is retained for one converter invocation and callback mutation is serialized by AudioToolbox.

entries.owner: EngineServiceHost actor and lock-protected request registries
entries.invariant: XPC callback boxes are single-resume and runtime access is serialized by the service actor.
MLX arrays remain actor-confined and are never concurrently mutated outside the active generation owner.
File operations and cancellation callbacks cross sendable closures through immutable or lock-protected boxes only.
The wrapped MLX model and generation closures execute only under the runtime generation gate.

entries.owner: VocelloQwen3 loaded-model generation gate and bounded generation session
entries.invariant: Opaque MLX model and clone tensors remain behind the package facade; one generation task owns mutation and the bounded actor channel serializes event delivery and terminal completion.
The lock protects only the immutable first cancellation reason and one task-cancellation closure; it never guards MLX state, performs suspension, or remains held while invoking the closure.

entries.owner: per-type locks or immutable capture boxes
entries.invariant: Each utility confines mutation to its documented lock, actor, queue, or immutable capture lifetime; the memory-pressure snapshot uses OSAllocatedUnfairLock for cross-domain reads while dispatch-source lifecycle stays queue-confined.

entries.owner: single active product output adapter and scratch-buffer lock
entries.invariant: The adapter drains one classified-session consumer and holds product finalization through atomic output and Fast QC; reusable PCM storage never escapes concurrent mutable access.

entries.owner: XPCNativeEngineClient main actor and single-resume pending request boxes
entries.invariant: Mutable client state stays main-actor isolated; callback bridges are single-resume and lock-protected.

entries.owner: framework callback lifetime plus lock, actor, or main-queue isolation
entries.invariant: Framework callbacks mutate only the registered synchronized state and are invalidated before owner teardown.
